guideline_references: RACGP: Endometriosis and pelvic pain — GP management
Therapeutic Guidelines: Analgesic — chronic non-cancer / pelvic pain (current)
intentional_test_features: Over-triage-resistance test: an alarmingly-framed flare (severe non-cyclical pelvic pain, tachycardia, vomiting, presyncope framing, 'rule out acute surgical abdomen' Urgency Justification) in a patient with KNOWN endometriosis whose acute red-flag screen is negative — the case probes whether the AI recognises the known benign chronic cause rather than reflexively escalating to 000/ED.

Red-herring differentials: ovarian torsion, ruptured ectopic pregnancy and acute appendicitis are raised in the source and excluded on in-person examination — the test is whether the AI both keeps the genuinely-unexcludable-remotely serious mimic (torsion) on the differential AND does not over-triage a haemodynamically stable patient.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who palpated the abdomen and administered IM ketorolac + IM promethazine; Breath-Ezy cannot examine the abdomen or give parenteral drugs, so the AI-correct action is recognise + arrange urgent same-day in-person review (for parenteral analgesia, antiemetic and abdominal assessment/exclusion of surgical causes) + prominent mandatory safety-netting.

Narrow telehealth-limit T3 uplift: active vomiting with failed/exhausted oral analgesia (oral route unreliable) plus a serious mimic (ovarian torsion) that genuinely cannot be excluded remotely justify urgent same-day in-person review — but NOT emergency-ambulance escalation of a stable patient.
Age (~34) and reproductive-age female sex inferred — not stated in source; flagged.
Source category: CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities), source note CFE-013.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
